forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure will persist across the coastal waters. The high pressure centre associated with the ridge will become established in the Tasman Sea over the weekend. A trough moves over the south early next week with a cold front clipping the southeast mid week. A new ridge develops south of the Bight early to mid next week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots north of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Clear.

South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending south to southwesterly in the middle of the day then tending south to southeasterly in the evening. Winds reaching up to 20 knots north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon and evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Sunny.
East to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the early morning then becoming south to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the early afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Sunny.
Variable about 10 knots becoming southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Sunny.
